audits.name: youtube-transcript
findings: Bash: needed for yt-dlp commands — justified
Write: needed to save transcript files — justified
Glob: needed to check input/ directory — justified
Read: needed to read VTT files for cleaning — justified
No disallowed_tools specified — should add Agent (skill doesn't need sub-agents)
Missing: Edit is not needed (always writes new files) — correctly excluded
recommendations: Add 'disallowedTools: Agent' to prevent accidental agent spawning

audits.name: autoloop
findings: All tools justified: Read/Write/Edit for file iteration, Glob/Grep for scoring, Bash for git operations
Agent explicitly disallowed — correct, this skill loops internally
Bash needed for git checkout/commit in iteration loop — justified
Write AND Edit both allowed — Write for new backup files, Edit for targeted skill changes — both justified
Broad set but each tool has clear purpose in the workflow
recommendations: No changes needed — permission level is correct given the autonomous iteration workflow

audits.name: budget
findings: Minimal toolset: Read/Write/Edit for budget.md operations — well-constrained
Agent disallowed — correct for a simple ledger manager
No Bash, Glob, Grep — correct, budget is a single-file operation
Best example of least-privilege in the skill set
recommendations: No changes needed — exemplary minimal permissions

audits.name: checkpoint
findings: Bash: needed for git status/log/diff commands — justified
Glob: needed to check for implementation-plan.md existence — justified
Grep: needed to search for today's learnings entries — justified
Agent disallowed — correct, checkpoint is a focused save operation
Write AND Edit both needed: Write for new checkpoint, Edit for clearing checkpoint — justified
Broad but all justified by the 'gather state from many sources' workflow

audits.name: dependency-audit
findings: WebSearch + WebFetch: core purpose of the skill — justified
Read: for requirements.txt and CLAUDE.md — justified
Glob: to find requirements files — justified
Grep: to search within dependency files — justified
No Write/Edit — correct, this skill only reports, doesn't modify
Agent disallowed — correct, all research done inline
Good least-privilege: no Bash, no Write, no unnecessary tools
recommendations: No changes needed — well-constrained for a read-and-report skill

audits.name: incident-runbook
findings: Write AND Edit explicitly disallowed — enforces the 'diagnose only, don't fix' mandate
Agent disallowed — keeps it fast and focused
Bash: for git log, process checks, port checking — justified
Read/Glob/Grep: for log inspection and runbook lookup — justified
Strong disallow list makes intent explicit — exemplary constraint design
recommendations: No changes needed — explicit disallow list is a good pattern worth following

audits.name: orchestrate
findings: Full toolset including Agent: justified — orchestrator must delegate work
No disallowed_tools — expected for the 'conductor' skill that needs full access
Write: for state.md, budget.md updates — justified
Bash: for git operations, context health checks — justified
This is the ONE skill that legitimately needs all tools
Broad permissions acceptable given orchestrator role — this is the trust boundary
recommendations: No changes needed — orchestrator is designed to be the privileged coordinator

audits.name: project-init
findings: Bash: needed for mkdir -p, git init — justified
Write: creates template files — justified
Read: checks existing files before overwriting — justified
Glob: checks for existing .claude/ structure — justified
Missing Edit: skill uses Write for new files, never needs to patch existing — correct omission
Agent disallowed — correct, initialization is a focused write operation
No Grep needed — correct, no content searching required

audits.name: scout
findings: Read/Glob/Grep: core exploration tools — justified
Agent: for deep tier cross-module mapping — justified
No Write/Edit/Bash: correct — scout must be read-only
No disallowed_tools specified — should explicitly disallow Write, Edit to enforce read-only mandate
The SKILL.md says 'never modify anything' but doesn't enforce it via disallowedTools
recommendations: Add 'disallowedTools: Write, Edit, Bash' to enforce read-only mandate — currently relies on instruction compliance only
Explicit disallow is safer than 'never modify' in text instructions

audits.name: scribe
findings: Minimal toolset: Read/Write/Edit for memory file management — correct
Agent disallowed — correct, scribe operates directly on files
No Bash, Glob, Grep — correct for a focused file writer
Write AND Edit both justified: Write for new archives, Edit for existing memory files

audits.name: skill-generator
findings: No Bash: correct — no shell operations needed for skill file management
Agent disallowed — correct, all work is file-based
Read/Write/Edit: for creating and updating SKILL.md files — justified
Glob/Grep: for listing existing skills and scanning content — justified
Good constraint: no Bash means no accidental execution of generated code

audits.name: verify
findings: Write AND Edit explicitly disallowed — enforces 'observe and report only' mandate
Bash: essential for running real commands and checking output — justified
Agent: for complex multi-component verification — justified
Read/Glob/Grep: for reading project context — justified
Strong disallow list + execution capability is the right balance for a verifier
recommendations: No changes needed — well-designed permission profile for a read+execute verifier

audits.name: watch
findings: Agent: listed as allowed but instructions say 'Never spawn more than 1 agent' — inconsistent
The instructions also say 'use haiku for agent spawns if deeper analysis needed' — so Agent is sometimes used
Agent permission is technically correct but the instructions cap it at 1
No disallowed_tools: should add Bash (not needed for web scanning), Glob (not needed)
Write/Edit: needed for news.md updates — justified
WebSearch/WebFetch: core purpose — justified
MINOR over-permission: Agent is justified but could be constrained further
recommendations: Consider adding: 'disallowedTools: Bash, Glob, Grep' — these tools have no role in web scanning
Agent permission is acceptable but instructions should clarify it's capped at 1 agent

audits.name: critic
findings: Write AND Edit explicitly disallowed — enforces 'never fix, only report' mandate
Bash: for git diff to get last changes — justified
Agent: for complex reviews (e.g., tracing call chains) — justified
Read/Glob/Grep: for code analysis — justified
Permission profile mirrors verify — both are read-only analysis tools
recommendations: No changes needed — correct permission profile for an analysis-only skill

audits.name: harness
findings: Full toolset including Agent — justified: harness spawns sub-agents for LLM phases
Bash: needed for creating .harness/ directory, running validation commands — justified
Write: for saving phase outputs to .harness/ — justified
No disallowedTools: acceptable for a dispatcher/runner that coordinates everything
Similar profile to orchestrate — both are coordinators that need full access
recommendations: Could consider adding disallowedTools for something like WebSearch/WebFetch that harness wouldn't typically need
